title: Dentist Open Saturday in Arlington, TX: What to Know
seoTitle: Dentist Open Saturday Arlington TX | What to Expect
seoDescription: Looking for a dentist open Saturday in Arlington TX? Here is what same-day weekend visits cover, what to bring, and how to book a slot.
author: Sonria Dentista clinical team
summary: A practical guide to finding a dentist open Saturday in Arlington, TX, including what same-day weekend visits typically handle, what to bring, and how to avoid driving across town only to be turned away.

body.text: Most dental offices in North Texas close after Friday at 5 pm. So when a tooth starts throbbing Saturday morning, finding a dentist open Saturday in Arlington, TX feels like a coin flip. This guide explains what is actually open on the weekend in Arlington, what kinds of issues a Saturday visit can solve, and what to bring so you do not waste the trip.

We are Sonria Dentista, and our Arlington office is open Saturday 9 am to 2 pm. Our Paris office is also open Saturday 9 am to 3 pm. So if you are reading this in pain on a Saturday, this article is for you.
Which dentists are actually open on Saturday in Arlington?

If you Google dentist open saturday arlington tx, you will see dozens of results, but many are misleading. Some clinics list Saturday hours but only book by appointment far in advance. Some list emergency Saturday but charge a $200 surcharge before they touch you. A few are actually open Saturday for routine and urgent care.
Before you drive over, call. A two-minute call can confirm:

items: They are actually open today (not just listed as open).
They have an opening in the next few hours.
Your insurance is in-network, or what your cash price would be.
They can handle your specific issue (some offices only do cleanings on weekends, no extractions or root canals).

body.title: Sonria Saturday hours
body.text: Arlington, TX: open Saturday 9 am to 2 pm. Paris, TX: open Saturday 9 am to 3 pm. Both locations handle exams, cleanings, fillings, extractions, and most emergencies. Call (682) 284-1120 for Arlington.
What can a Saturday dental visit actually cover?

A common worry is that Saturday means a stripped-down clinic with only a few services. At a real Saturday office, the chair, the X-ray machine, and the tools are the same as during the week. The only thing that might be limited is the lab. Same-day crowns, for example, depend on the in-office mill being staffed.
Cleanings and exams
If you work Monday through Friday, Saturday is the easiest time to do your six-month cleaning. Plan for 60 to 90 minutes.
Fillings
Tooth-colored composite fillings can be done in one Saturday visit, usually 45 to 60 minutes. If you have a small cavity, a Saturday slot is often enough to finish it.
Toothache and pain relief

If you are in pain, the Saturday visit is about identifying the cause and stopping the pain. That usually means an X-ray, a quick exam, and either a temporary filling, an extraction, or starting antibiotics if there is an abscess.
Tooth extractions
Simple extractions take 20 to 40 minutes. Surgical extractions (broken tooth, impacted root) take longer and may need scheduling for a weekday if the tooth is complex.
Lost or broken crowns and fillings
If a crown popped off Friday night while you were eating, we can almost always cement it back on the same Saturday morning if you bring the crown with you.
Sports trauma and chipped teeth
Saturday morning sports leagues mean Saturday morning chipped teeth. We see a lot of these. A small chip can be repaired with composite in 30 minutes.
What might not be possible on a Saturday?
Some procedures need a longer block, a specialist, or a fully staffed lab, and those are typically scheduled for a weekday:

items: Root canals on molars (these can take 90 minutes and benefit from a fully staffed office).
Implant placement (the surgery is usually scheduled when the surgical team is full-staffed).
Same-day crowns (depends on whether the in-office lab is open that Saturday).
Orthodontics adjustments (some offices only do these on weekdays).

body.text: Even if the final fix is on a weekday, a Saturday visit can stop the pain, take the X-ray, and get you on antibiotics if needed. That alone is worth the trip.
What to bring to a Saturday dental visit
If you are calling Saturday morning trying to get in same-day, you do not have time to mail forms. Show up with:

items: Your dental insurance card if you have one.
A list of medications you take, especially blood thinners.
If a crown or filling fell out, the piece itself in a small bag.
If you had a knocked-out tooth, the tooth in milk.

body.text: If you do not have insurance, that is fine. We have a $79.99 new patient special that covers a full exam, X-rays, and consultation. Many Saturday emergencies fit inside that price plus the cost of whatever procedure you actually need.
How much does a Saturday dental visit cost?
Some offices charge a Saturday emergency surcharge ($75 to $200) on top of the procedure. We do not. Saturday pricing at Sonria is the same as Monday pricing. Examples for cash patients in 2026:

items: Limited emergency exam plus targeted X-ray: $89 to $149.

body.text: If you have insurance, your share is usually 0 to 20% of these numbers depending on your plan.
Why are Saturday slots so hard to get in Arlington?

Two reasons. First, the supply: only a handful of practices in Arlington are open Saturday. Second, the demand: working families schedule routine cleanings on Saturday, so the slots fill 1 to 2 weeks out. By Saturday morning, the calendar is mostly booked, leaving only emergency walk-in slots.
Two practical tips: book your routine Saturday cleaning at least two weeks ahead, and if you wake up in pain on a Saturday, call by 9 am. The earlier you call, the more likely a same-day slot exists.
Saturday vs the ER for dental pain

If you cannot get a Saturday dentist and your tooth is in extreme pain, an urgent care or ER will handle the pain (usually with antibiotics and a prescription pain reliever) but will not fix the underlying problem. ER dental visits run $400 to $1,200 and end with you needing to see a dentist anyway. A Saturday dentist visit at $150 to $400 solves the actual problem and skips the ER bill. Save the ER for true emergencies (uncontrolled bleeding, facial swelling that is affecting breathing or vision, or jaw trauma).
Frequently asked questions
Do you need an appointment, or can you walk in?

Walk-ins on Saturday in Arlington can work if you arrive right at 9 am. After 10 am the schedule is usually full. We always recommend a quick call first so we can hold a slot for you. Call (682) 284-1120.
Are Saturday dental visits more expensive?
At some clinics, yes. Saturday surcharges of $75 to $200 are common. We do not charge a Saturday surcharge at Sonria. Same prices, same insurance coverage, same team.
What if I just need a cleaning, not an emergency?
Saturday cleanings are very popular at our Arlington office for working families. We recommend booking 1 to 2 weeks ahead. The whole family can come the same day.
Are kids welcome on Saturday?
Yes. We see kids starting at the first tooth. Saturday is actually one of our most popular times for family blocks where parents and kids come in together.
Need a Saturday slot today?

Sonria Arlington is open Saturday 9 am to 2 pm. Paris is open Saturday 9 am to 3 pm. Bilingual team, transparent pricing, and same-day care for most issues. Call (682) 284-1120 to book Arlington or to ask which location can see you fastest.
